reports.title: Trump Eyes New Iran Strikes as Oil Hits $126
reports.subtitle: US Central Command prepares a fresh wave of strikes while a fragile ceasefire is still technically in place.

reports.title: SCOTUS Strikes Down Louisiana Map, Gutting Voting Rights Act
reports.subtitle: The court rules majority-minority districts unconstitutional, handing Republicans a redistricting weapon heading into the midterms.

reports.title: Musk Takes the Stand Against OpenAI, Claims Altman Stole a Charity
reports.subtitle: Day three of the trial reveals a fight not about AI safety but about who controls the most valuable organization in Silicon Valley.

reports.title: Powell's Last Stand: The Fed Holds Rates and Its Ground
reports.subtitle: Jerome Powell holds rates steady in what is expected to be his final meeting as Fed chair, warning that legal attacks are battering the institution.

reports.title: Big Tech Earns More, Spends More on AI. Meta Investors Revolt.
reports.subtitle: Alphabet's 63% cloud growth contrasts with Meta's share drop after raising AI capex to $145 billion with no clear payoff plan.

reports.title: UAE Quits OPEC After 60 Years, and Saudi Arabia Has No Answer
reports.subtitle: Abu Dhabi's defection doesn't change oil prices today, but it signals that the cartel's era of shared discipline is over.

reports.title: Israel Seizes Gaza Aid Flotilla 1,000 Miles From Gaza
reports.subtitle: The interception near Crete ends the mission before it starts and turns 175 detained activists into a diplomatic problem for every government that said nothing.

reports.title: Kevin Warsh Clears Senate Banking Committee. The Fed's Independence Window Is Closing.
reports.subtitle: The party-line 13-11 vote advances a nominee who pledged Fed independence but whose confirmation is a direct reward for Trump's months-long pressure campaign against Powell.

reports.title: Trump Pays $2 Billion in Taxpayer Money to Kill Wind Farms
reports.subtitle: The administration is using federal funds to buy out offshore wind contracts it could not cancel legally, turning a policy reversal into a taxpayer liability.

reports.title: Saudi Arabia Is Cutting LIV Golf Loose. The Sportswashing Bet Failed.
reports.subtitle: After three years and billions spent buying defections and legitimacy, Riyadh is walking away from a tour that never became a sport.

reports.title: Putin Proposes a Victory Day Ceasefire. Ukraine Calls It a Parade.
reports.subtitle: Russia says Ukraine's consent isn't required. Kyiv says Moscow just wants a safe backdrop for its military parade.

reports.title: House GOP Ends the DHS Shutdown by Cutting ICE Funding. Both Sides Claim Victory.
reports.subtitle: After 75 days, House Republicans reopened TSA and the Coast Guard -- but passed zero dollars for immigration enforcement to get it done.

reports.title: EU AI Talks Collapse at Midnight. August Deadline Is Back.
reports.subtitle: Twelve hours of Brussels negotiations failed over industrial exemptions, leaving 50,000 European companies without clarity on whether they need to comply in 98 days.

reports.title: Inflation Hits a 3-Year High. The Fed Cannot Cut. The Strait Is Still Closed.
reports.subtitle: The PCE gauge jumped to 3.5% in March as the Iran war pushed gas prices to record highs. The Fed is trapped: it cannot cut into rising inflation and cannot raise into a 2% GDP economy.

reports.title: Supreme Court Signals It Will End Deportation Protections for 1.3 Million Migrants
reports.subtitle: A conservative majority appeared ready Wednesday to let Trump strip TPS from Haitians and Syrians -- and potentially from all 17 countries whose nationals hold the status.

reports.title: DeepSeek Bets on Huawei and China's Chip Independence Finally Gets a Proof of Concept
reports.subtitle: After years of Huawei's Ascend chips failing to attract top-tier customers, DeepSeek V4 just gave Beijing's domestic silicon strategy its first credible validation.

reports.title: Amazon Fires 30,000. Then Announces 11,000 New Hires. The CEO Says AI Isn't Replacing Anyone.
reports.subtitle: The numbers don't add up on purpose: Amazon is running two contradictory stories simultaneously because each one serves a different audience.

reports.title: US and China Trade Barbs Two Weeks Before the Summit. Both Sides Are Building Leverage, Not Trust.
reports.subtitle: Bessent calls Beijing's new supply chain rules 'provocative' on the same day He Lifeng voices 'solemn concern' over US trade measures. The Trump-Xi meeting is 15 days away.

reports.title: Instagram Cracks Down on Reposts the Same Week ADL Finds It Fails to Remove 93% of Hate Content
reports.subtitle: Meta is enforcing its content rules selectively: original creators get protection, extremist networks get a pass.

reports.title: US Jobless Claims Hit a 57-Year Low. Economists Say the Real Pain Is Still Coming.
reports.subtitle: At 189,000, initial claims are the lowest since September 1969. Analysts say tariffs and Iran war costs haven't hit payrolls yet, but they will.
